problem_statement: The project currently targets Python 3.12 (pinned in both Dockerfiles and pyproject.toml) and LangGraph >=0.2. The issue requests migration to the latest stable Python (3.13) and latest LangGraph/LangChain release. Note: the project has no direct `langchain` dependency — only `langgraph>=0.2` — so 'langchain' in the issue title refers to the LangGraph ecosystem upgrade.
acceptance_criteria: Dockerfile and Dockerfile.webhook use `python:3.13-slim` as base image
All existing unit, integration, and e2e tests pass under Python 3.13
CI pipeline (`build-analyzer-image` job) builds successfully with the new base image
Deprecated LangGraph 0.2 API `set_entry_point()` in `src/pipeline/graph.py` is replaced with `add_edge(START, 'gather_context')` per LangGraph 0.3+ idiom
`from langgraph.graph import START` is imported alongside `END` in `src/pipeline/graph.py`
technical_approach: 1. Audit current LangGraph changelog for breaking changes from 0.2 to latest: focus on `StateGraph` API, checkpoint/interrupt mechanics, and any import path renames

2. Update `pyproject.toml`: set `requires-python = '>=3.13'` and bump `langgraph` lower-bound to the latest stable (e.g. `>=0.3`); add `langchain-core` only if LangGraph now requires it as an explicit peer
5. Fix deprecated API in `src/pipeline/graph.py:1`: add `START` to the `langgraph.graph` import

7. Verify `asyncio.run()` usage in sync LangGraph nodes (`src/pipeline/nodes.py:57,79,102,249,270`) is still supported — Python 3.13 tightened event-loop semantics, and LangGraph 0.3 may prefer async nodes natively
8. Run full test suite: `pytest tests/unit tests/integration` in a Python 3.13 virtualenv with updated deps
9. Build Docker image locally and smoke-test pipeline entrypoint (`python -m src.pipeline.main`) with a mock issue
dependencies: langgraph (PyPI) — direct runtime dep; confirm latest stable tag before pinning
langchain-core (PyPI) — indirect dep through langgraph; may need explicit version floor if LangGraph drops its re-export
pydantic>=2.7 — Python 3.13 compatibility confirmed in Pydantic 2.7+; no change expected
fastapi>=0.111 — verify 3.13 wheel availability; FastAPI 0.115+ ships 3.13 wheels
python:3.13-slim Docker image — must be available on Docker Hub at build time in GitLab CI

risks.description: `asyncio.run()` called inside synchronous LangGraph node functions (nodes.py:57, 79, 102, 249, 270) may conflict with LangGraph 0.3's internal event loop if the graph runner is itself async
risks.mitigation: Convert node functions to `async def` and use `await` directly, or verify LangGraph 0.3 still supports sync nodes with nested `asyncio.run()` calls before merging

risks.description: LangGraph 0.3+ may rename or restructure internal modules (e.g. checkpoint, pregel) causing import errors not caught by the current test suite
risks.mitigation: Run `pip install langgraph==<target>` in a fresh venv and do a full import check (`python -c 'from langgraph.graph import StateGraph, START, END'`) before updating the constraint

risks.description: Python 3.13 removes `typing.get_type_hints` quirks and tightens `__slots__` on dataclasses; Pydantic models in `src/models/` could fail validation silently or loudly
risks.mitigation: Run the unit tests (`tests/unit/test_models.py`) explicitly under Python 3.13 and compare Pydantic model serialization output

risks.description: `python:3.13-slim` base image may lack a pre-built wheel for one of the native-extension deps (e.g. `uvicorn[standard]`), causing build-time failures in CI
risks.mitigation: Test `docker build` locally before pushing; pin to `python:3.13.x-slim` with an explicit patch version for reproducibility

open_questions: Is the intended LangGraph target the latest stable release at migration time, or a specific version (e.g. 0.3.x)? The issue says 'latest' but CI images should be pinned.
Should `langchain` (langchain-core / langchain) be added as an explicit direct dependency, or is `langgraph` alone sufficient? The issue title says 'langchain' but the codebase only uses langgraph.
Do the LangGraph node functions need to be converted to `async def` as part of this migration, or is that out of scope for this issue?
Is Python 3.14 (currently in beta as of June 2026) in scope, or is Python 3.13 the target latest stable?
